summary: External-review improvement cycle for CC-0080: the unexported isMissingSecretOrKey helper at operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_dbconnection_secret.go has been moved to internal/common/secrets/secrets.go and exported as IsMissingSecretOrKey, with the two call sites in reconcile_dbconnection_secret.go updated and the now-unused errors import dropped. Planwerk metadata files (progress phase, review pattern occurrence count, external review verdict) are updated accordingly. The change precisely addresses gndrmnn's request and correctly applies the 'use shared helpers instead of inline reimplementation' review pattern.

tests_checklist.item: go build passes for changed packages
go vet passes for changed packages
Existing unit tests still pass (internal/common/secrets, operators/keystone/internal/controller)
Call-site behaviour (upstream Secret missing → SecretsReady=False, requeue) remains covered by TestReconcileDBConnectionSecret_UpstreamSecretMissing_RequeueAndCondition
IsMissingSecretOrKey has a dedicated unit test in secrets_test.go
Missing-data-key branch (upstream Secret exists but lacks 'username'/'password' key) is exercised by a reconciler test

code_quality_checklist.item: Helper exported with clear, accurate godoc referencing CC-0080 and W-001
Unused errors import removed after extraction
Both call sites consistently use secrets.IsMissingSecretOrKey (no stragglers)
No other callers of GetSecretValue in the codebase that would benefit from the shared helper

security_checklist.item: No new secret material introduced; refactor is behaviour-preserving

architecture_checklist.item: Helper lives in the shared secrets package next to the sentinel (ErrKeyNotFound) and the function that wraps it (GetSecretValue) — single source of truth
No cross-cutting abstractions introduced beyond what the task required

dry_yagni_checklist.item: Duplication between operator packages prevented by centralising the helper
No speculative generality (helper signature is minimal: func(error) bool)

fail_fast_checklist.item: nil-guard returns early (false) before invoking apierrors.IsNotFound

defensive_checklist.item: Handles nil, IsNotFound, and wrapped ErrKeyNotFound uniformly

issues_found.description: The newly exported IsMissingSecretOrKey has no direct unit test. It is indirectly exercised via reconcileDBConnectionSecret (upstream-missing path), but as a public API of the shared secrets package it should have its own table-driven test covering: nil input → false, apierrors.NewNotFound-wrapped error → true, fmt.Errorf("...: %w", ErrKeyNotFound) → true, arbitrary error → false. This protects the contract if GetSecretValue's wrapping ever changes.
issues_found.fix: Add TestIsMissingSecretOrKey with the four cases above next to the existing GetSecretValue tests.

issues_found.description: reconcile_dbconnection_secret_test.go covers the upstream-Secret-missing case but does not exercise the missing-data-key branch (Secret exists, 'username' or 'password' key absent). The two call sites at reconcile_dbconnection_secret.go:63 and :81 share identical logic but the ErrKeyNotFound branch of IsMissingSecretOrKey is not reached by any controller-level test.
issues_found.fix: Add a test that seeds an upstream Secret with only 'username' (password missing) and asserts WaitingForDBCredentials + RequeueAfter, matching the existing missing-Secret test.

suggested_improvements: Add a dedicated unit test for secrets.IsMissingSecretOrKey to lock in its contract independently of GetSecretValue's wrapping details.
Add a controller-level test that exercises the missing-data-key branch in reconcileDBConnectionSecret so both error paths of IsMissingSecretOrKey are covered by integration-style tests.
next_steps: Optional: add the two minor tests above; the refactor itself is ready to merge.
